literature-report-plan.name: R3-biomedical-segmentation

metadata.title: Biomedical Segmentation: Tubular Structures, Foundation Models, and Statistical Evaluation

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Biomedical Segmentation: Tubular Structures, Foundation Models, and Statistical Evaluation"
Focus: THREE interleaved threads:
A) TUBULAR SEGMENTATION: Vessel/tubular structure segmentation methods. clDice, centerline dice, topology-aware losses (cbDice, boundary losses). MONAI's role and what could be contributed TO MONAI. Current SOTA for vessel segmentation in microscopy and clinical imaging.
B) FOUNDATION MODELS: How SAM3, VesselFM, and medical VLMs/MLLMs are changing segmentation. Calibration/UQ for foundation models specifically. Zero-shot vs fine-tuned performance gaps. LoRA/adapter approaches.
C) BIOSTATISTICAL EVALUATION: What methods are researchers using to compare segmentation models? Can you find factorial design papers in medical imaging? Sample size issues when voxels are not independent observations. TRIPOD+AI, PROBAST+AI, STARD-AI reporting guidelines. Riley et al. prediction model evaluation framework. Multiverse analysis for segmentation.
Context: Our repo runs a 4-model × 3-loss factorial design with 3-fold CV on 70 volumes. We need to justify this statistically and compare with what the field is doing.
EXCLUDED topics: - MLOps infrastructure details (report R2) - Reproducibility crisis background (report R1) - Microscopy hardware acquisition (report R4) - Agentic AI (report 5)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: training, models, architecture Manuscript section: Methods (all of R2), Results (R3a loss ablation, R3b multi-model)

seed-papers.paper: Maier-Hein et al. (2024). "Metrics Reloaded." Nature Methods 21, 195-212. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-023-02151-z
Collins et al. (2024). "TRIPOD+AI Statement." BMJ 385. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj-2023-078378
Riley et al. (2024). "Evaluation of Clinical Prediction Models (Part 1)." BMJ 384. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj-2023-074819
Riley & Collins (2023). "Stability of Clinical Prediction Models." Biometrical Journal. https://doi.org/10.1002/bimj.202200302
Riley et al. (2023). "Clinical Prediction Models and the Multiverse of Madness." BMC Medicine 21, 502. https://doi.org/10.1186/s12916-023-03212-y
Van Calster et al. (2025). "Performance Measures in Predictive AI." Lancet Digital Health. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.landig.2025.100916
Kofler et al. (2023). "Are we using appropriate segmentation metrics?" arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.07396
Shit et al. (2021). "clDice - a Novel Topology-Preserving Loss Function." CVPR. https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.07311
Poon et al. (2023). "A dataset of rodent cerebrovasculature." Scientific Data 10, 141. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-023-02048-8
Pollard et al. (2026). "TRIPOD-Code." Diagnostic and Prognostic Research 10. https://doi.org/10.1186/s41512-025-00217-4
Legha et al. (2026). "Sequential Sample Size Calculations." JCE 191. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclinepi.2025.112117

search-domains.domain: SAM3 medical segmentation foundation model fine-tuning 2025
calibration uncertainty quantification foundation model segmentation
factorial design medical image segmentation comparison 2024
sample size segmentation study voxel-level non-independent 2024
MONAI segmentation contribution open source medical 2025
